body: Kept hunting per your instruction. Checked the same two defect classes that produced WARRANT#97/98 (cascading gates, computed-but-discarded stats) against the other highest-stakes checks in the file: the bracket-recheck fallback, the ratchet's own promotion gate (ForgeRatchetPromote), and Phase 5/6's retry loops. All four are genuinely already sound -- the ratchet promotion gate in particular is one of the most rigorously enforced functions in the whole file (PBO bar, EV-improvement bar, non-inferiority on 4 separate guardrails, per-regime regression check, AND the real deflated-Sharpe screen, all actually gating, not just logged). Phase 5 and Phase 6 both already got upgraded from 2 retries to 5-8 retries in an earlier pass, long before tonight. So: real plateau reached for this specific angle -- zero new code changes this pass, and I'm reporting that honestly rather than manufacturing marginal fixes just to keep shipping warrants. The one thing still standing from the backlog is the multiple-testing correction on the raw 562,500-combination search space itself (not the gates around it) -- that's a different, bigger undertaking needing a full read of the search implementation first. Ledger at pass_79 (audit-only, no compile needed).
